title: The Silk Roads
subtitle: A New History of the World
author: Peter Frankopan
themes: World history from a Central Asian, not European, vantage point
The Silk Roads as a network of connections shaping global civilization
How the East shaped the West, often more than the reverse
Continuity of exchange, faith, and power along the Eurasian corridor
The twentieth and twenty-first centuries as a new silk-roads era driven by oil and infrastructure
awards: Sunday Times History Book of the Year (2015)
The Daily Telegraph History Book of the Year (2015)
Bild am Sonntag Non-Fiction Book of the Year (2016)
rationale: Selected as Book #2 for an EMPTY leaf. Challenges Eurocentric world history by reframing the Silk Roads corridor as the real center of global affairs for 2,500 years. Pairs with GGS by Hemisphere/deframes it geographically.
excerpt: For two thousand years the center of the world sat east of the Mediterranean, not west of it. Peter Frankopan reframes world history around the Silk Roads — the corridor of land and sea that linked China, India, Persia, the steppe, the Middle East, and Europe — and argues that almost every major transformation in human affairs was shaped there long before the West claimed the spotlight.
